prompt: Cinematic wide-angle view inside a large auditorium from an elevated rear corner, the rows of seats are not simply missing but progressively distorted as they descend toward the stage, in the back, seats are intact and tightly arranged, moving forward, rows begin to stretch, tilt, and warp as if pulled by an invisible force, near the front, nearly 40% of the seating area has been consumed into a dark, undefined void, with remaining seats elongated and thinning before disappearing entirely, the floor subtly curves downward into the void, and architectural lines bend toward it, the ceiling above also sags slightly in the same direction, reinforcing a unified gravitational pull, a single dim spotlight falls on one intact seat near the back, surrounded by growing emptiness, mood: irreversible, collapsing, quietly catastrophic, color palette: cold desaturated blues, charcoal greys, deep shadowed blacks, lighting: low, directional, with soft falloff into darkness toward the void, composition: asymmetrical, with the void dominating the lower center, perspective lines dragging the viewer’s eye forward into absence, no symmetry, no grid-like regularity, no ice, no water, no environmental symbols, no abandoned-building clichés, no text, no labels, no legends, no axes, no numbers visible in image
insight: Arctic September sea ice extent has declined by about 13% per decade since 1979, meaning over 40% of the summer ice has disappeared within a human lifetime.
